114572.explanation: We find it important that residents think along and participate. This can be done through good participation and input upfront. A referendum is not always the best tool and can oversimplify complex choices.

114573.explanation: If residents have a good plan, they should be able to take over municipal tasks. This increases involvement and ownership. The municipality supports and remains responsible for clear agreements and quality.

114575.explanation: Current policy works well. Pubs and restaurants have clear opening hours, which provides calm and clarity. At the same time, there is room for customization, as entrepreneurs can deviate on special occasions.

114576.explanation: Generating sustainable energy together is good for the climate and the neighborhood. Residents who want to do this together deserve support from the municipality. This makes the energy transition fair and achievable for everyone.

114578.explanation: People fleeing deserve safe shelter. As a municipality, we take our responsibility. Good reception, with attention to support, community acceptance, and rapid integration, creates calm and prevents problems.

114580.explanation: Fireplaces and wood stoves cause air pollution and health complaints. We opt for clean air and a healthy living environment. Therefore, we want to limit their use and promote cleaner, sustainable alternatives. The 'stookwijzer' (burning guide) can be a first aid tool.

114582.explanation: A municipal alternative with lights or drones is safe and festive. It causes less nuisance, less damage, and less stress for people and animals. This way, we celebrate New Year's Eve together in a modern and responsible way.

114585.explanation: Everyone must be able to independently visit shops, cafes, and other buildings. By partially financially helping entrepreneurs with adaptations, we make Goes more accessible to people with disabilities and ensure everyone can participate.

114587.explanation: The municipality adheres to what is legally agreed upon for the local broadcaster. We find that sufficient.

114591.explanation: The number of BOAs (special enforcement officers) has already increased significantly during the past council term, partly thanks to GroenLinks-PvdA. More BOAs would cost a lot of money. We prefer to spend that money on prevention, youth work, education, and equal opportunities.

114592.explanation: Camera surveillance is not always the best solution. We prioritize prevention, visible enforcement, and strong neighborhoods first. Temporary camera surveillance can only be used in places where it is truly necessary.

114594.explanation: BOAs have an important role, but arming them does not fit that role. We choose de-escalation, good training, and cooperation with the police. Safety is achieved not by more weapons but through prevention and trust.

114595.explanation: Social rental housing is for those who need it most. We do not consider priority based on place of residence to be fair. We choose allocation based on necessity, so everyone has an equal chance of finding suitable housing.

114597.explanation: Homes are meant for living in, not for buying to sell on for profit. With an owner-occupancy obligation, we tackle unscrupulous landlords and ensure owner-occupied homes remain available for people who want to live there themselves.

114598.explanation: Converting vacant office buildings into housing is a good solution. We want to first speed up procedures and remove obstacles. A subsidy can help, but only as a last resort to make projects possible.

114601.explanation: Waiving municipal taxes helps people with low incomes to make ends meet. Stopping this increases financial problems and debts. We choose basic security and support for those who need it.

114602.explanation: Poverty schemes are important to enable participation and prevent debts. We are raising the threshold to 130% of the social assistance standard. In addition, we want new policy with a gradual reduction of assistance if your income is between 130% and 150%, so earning more pays off and working poor people also receive support.

114604.explanation: Driving electric helps to make the air cleaner. More charging points are needed to make this possible for everyone. We do this in consultation with residents, so it fits the neighborhood and the public space is used well.

114605.explanation: Within the built-up area, we choose 30 km/h to make streets safer and calmer. This aligns with the STOMP principle: first walking, cycling and public transport, then the car. On important main routes, 50 km/h will remain possible.

114608.explanation: Care works best when people feel understood and safe. Therefore, there must be room to choose a caregiver that fits a person's religion, culture or beliefs, as long as this is feasible and the quality of care remains good.

114609.explanation: Quality must come first in youth care. Focusing too much on price can compromise good care. We choose appropriate help, consistent caregivers and expertise, so children and families get the support they need.

114610.explanation: Menstrual products are basic necessities. Not everyone can always afford them. That is why we provide free tampons and sanitary pads. This has already been arranged during the past council term, partly thanks to GroenLinks-PvdA.

114613.explanation: Tourism is important for the local economy but also puts extra pressure on facilities. With a higher tourist tax, we make visitors contribute to this. The proceeds are used for liveability and maintenance in Goes.

114616.explanation: Refugees with a residence permit (status holders) may stay in the Netherlands. Priority in social housing enables them to work, learn and integrate faster into society. This relieves the shelters and prevents unnecessary costs.

114618.explanation: We want to generate sustainable energy but also handle agricultural land and landscape carefully. Therefore, we prioritize solar panels on roofs and unused land. We prefer to generate energy where it is used. Solar panels on agricultural land are allowed only after careful consideration.

114619.explanation: The Historic Museum of the Bevelanden tells the story of our region and is important for culture and education. Support from the municipality helps preserve this heritage and keep it accessible to residents and visitors.

114626.explanation: Paid parking helps keep the center accessible and livable. Lower rates lead to more car traffic and less space for greenery and recreation. We prefer to encourage walking, cycling and public transport, with good alternatives for parking and a transferium on the edge of town.

114628.explanation: A hotel tower of 55 meters does not fit the area near Deltaweg. Such a building has a major impact on the landscape and liveability. We adhere to clear height agreements and choose development that fits the scale of Goes.

114629.explanation: A new residential area is already being developed in Wilhelminadorp and with other plans we will build enough homes until 2030. We also want space for nature, such as the Kloetinge Landscape Park. A new location will only be considered if housing, nature and liveability have been weighed together.

114632.explanation: Culture brings people together and makes Goes vibrant. By investing in culture, we ensure everyone can participate regardless of income. That strengthens social interaction, talent development and the attractiveness of our city and villages.

114572.explanation: Nieuw Goes was founded to give residents more influence. Introducing a municipal referendum is the ultimate way to achieve this. It is one of our main positions.

114573.explanation: We are in favor of the 'right to challenge'. This gives residents the right to challenge the municipality to perform tasks more cheaply. This saves the municipality money and gives residents room to undertake.

114575.explanation: There must be clear closing times for cafés in the nightlife. Residents in the city center also deserve peace. However, we do think more nightlife venues should be allowed to stay open until 4 a.m. This prevents long queues and nuisance. Young people need things to do!

114576.explanation: It depends on the method and the percentage the municipality contributes. We are not in favor of huge wind turbines in the landscape, even if residents want to finance them.

114578.explanation: Large-scale shelter can cause large-scale problems both for asylum seekers and for the Goes community. Think of nuisance and unsafe situations, but also mental complaints among asylum seekers. Small-scale emergency shelter is possible if residents agree. Otherwise not.

114580.explanation: Nieuw Goes thinks burning fireplaces and wood stoves in rural areas should simply be allowed. If real nuisance occurs in a specific area, we support bans in those areas.

114582.explanation: While we support banning fireworks for private individuals, we are not negative about organizing a fireworks display in Goes or other replacements, such as a laser show. However, this must be affordable and the location must be considered to avoid nuisance to animals.

114585.explanation: With a subsidy for entrepreneurs with limited means, we would like to make this possible. Goes must be accessible for everyone!

114587.explanation: Goes already supports our local broadcaster Streekomroep de Bevelanden. We want to maintain this to give local talent space to develop.

114591.explanation: Nieuw Goes has succeeded in expanding the BOA team in Goes. We believe the team is still not large enough. There should be more BOAs to address nuisance.

114592.explanation: We do not consider camera surveillance a solution. It violates residents' privacy. More eyes on the street through more BOAs and police is a better solution for nuisance.

114594.explanation: BOAs must be able to defend themselves if they are attacked by troublemakers.

114595.explanation: We find it important that our own residents can always continue living in their own environment. Here they have many social contacts and possibly their work.

114597.explanation: Nieuw Goes opposes resale of homes for profit. You can counter this by introducing this measure. The housing shortage in the Netherlands is serious and must be addressed.

114598.explanation: It should be possible to convert vacant office buildings in certain locations.

114601.explanation: Waiving municipal taxes for residents with low incomes should remain possible. Otherwise, (financial) problems will continue to pile up with no solution in sight.

114602.explanation: Income thresholds should be applied more gradually so that benefits do not disappear all at once as you earn more. This should be phased out in steps the more you earn. In addition, working must pay off!

114604.explanation: Only if there is demand for it. At the moment, this does not seem to be the case.

114605.explanation: Goes is a compact city with a lot of traffic.

114608.explanation: Care should be neutral in terms of religion, culture, or belief. More important is to address the shortages in healthcare. More general practitioners and dentists in Goes would be a good start.

114609.explanation: The balance between quality and price is important. The cheapest option does not always provide what is necessary for good youth care.

114610.explanation: It must be possible for people living in poverty to receive free tampons and sanitary pads.

114613.explanation: In Goes, the tourist tax is low compared to other Zeeland municipalities. When tourists recreate here, they use our facilities. These are becoming increasingly expensive, so tourists should also pay more.

114616.explanation: Our own residents have difficulty finding housing. It is important that local residents can more easily find a home here. They have their friends, family, and often work here. Giving priority to refugees with residence permits counters this.

114618.explanation: There is enough space for solar panels on roofs or in verges and pieces of land not suitable for agriculture. It is not necessary to sacrifice agricultural land for solar panels.

114619.explanation: The municipality of Goes should examine possibilities to revive the museum. For tourism, it is important that we have a full-fledged museum about our local history and culture.

114626.explanation: Affordable parking close to the center is important to maintain the retail sector in the city center. Parking is currently very expensive. It should be possible to introduce day rates in Goes municipality for the shopping area when people come for a day of shopping. The Molenplein should be free of charge.

114628.explanation: We find the location along the Deltaweg unsuitable. It is too close to the Poelbos.

114629.explanation: Nieuw Goes still sees opportunities for new homes in villages. We also want to develop 'Mannee-Zuid' between Goes East and Kloetinge; a new neighborhood with lots of greenery and respect for the landscape.

114632.explanation: In the field of culture, funds can be better managed by financing fixed institutions more securely. In addition, there should be more offers for youth and tourists.

114572.explanation: CDA Goes chooses to listen and cooperate with residents through conversations and participation. A referendum simplifies complex choices and, in our view, does not contribute to good and careful governance.

114573.explanation: Residents often know well what works in their neighborhood. According to CDA Goes, the municipality should give residents space to manage facilities and take initiatives, with trust as a principle.

114575.explanation: Clear opening hours, according to CDA Goes, provide calm, safety, and a level playing field. Completely free hours can lead to nuisance and uncertainty for residents and entrepreneurs.

114576.explanation: CDA Goes encourages sustainability, but it must remain affordable for the municipality. Financial support is not always appropriate and is primarily a task of the national government.

114578.explanation: Asylum reception requires compassion and taking responsibility. That is why CDA Goes opts for manageable asylum reception with attention to safety, support, and good guidance.

114580.explanation: CDA Goes believes wood burning should remain permitted within existing regulations. We focus on conscious and responsible use, not on a total ban without support.

114582.explanation: Fireworks can bring people together, but according to CDA Goes, they do not always have to be organized by the municipality. Initiatives from neighborhoods or associations fit well with our vision.

114585.explanation: Accessibility is important to CDA Goes, but it remains primarily the responsibility of building owners. The municipality can encourage and advise, but we do not choose to provide structural subsidies.

114587.explanation: The local broadcaster is important to CDA Goes, but structural financing is, in our view, chiefly a task for the national government. The municipality will continue to cooperate and support where possible, as is already the case with regional broadcaster De Bevelanden.

114591.explanation: More visible enforcement increases safety and trust, according to CDA Goes. Public order officers ensure compliance with rules and a pleasant living environment in neighborhoods and the city center.

114592.explanation: Camera surveillance helps prevent nuisance and crime. CDA Goes wants the municipality to use this carefully, with respect for privacy.

114594.explanation: CDA Goes believes public order officers must be able to do their jobs safely. Proper resources are part of a strong approach to nuisance and crime.

114595.explanation: People who live or work here should have a chance at housing in their own municipality, according to CDA Goes. Connection with Goes counts for us!

114597.explanation: CDA Goes believes houses are for living in, not for speculation. A owner-occupancy requirement helps starters and families find affordable housing.

114598.explanation: Repurposing can help, but subsidies are not always necessary. CDA Goes wants the municipality to facilitate where needed but leave the initiative to market parties.

114601.explanation: CDA Goes wants to keep housing affordable in Goes for residents with low incomes. Waivers prevent people with low incomes from falling into further trouble.

114602.explanation: CDA Goes opts for customized solutions and early assistance. A general increase is not always effective or financially sustainable.

114604.explanation: Electric driving is growing. More charging stations are necessary, according to CDA Goes, to make sustainability practical and accessible.

114605.explanation: Maintain 30 km/h for residential streets, but keep 50 km/h on arterial roads, such as the Ringbaan and the Oranjeweg. This prevents shortcut traffic through the neighborhood. Good flow on main roads is, in CDA Goes' view, the best guarantee for safety and peace in residential areas.

114608.explanation: CDA Goes believes people should be able to choose care that fits their religion, culture, or conviction. That leads to appropriate care and respect for the person.

114609.explanation: Good care is CDA Goes' top priority, with attention also to costs. So we seek a balance between quality and affordability.

114610.explanation: CDA Goes wants the municipality to help people with financial worries through customized solutions and various existing schemes. That is more effective than general distribution.

114613.explanation: Tourists make use of the many facilities Goes offers. Asking them a fair contribution helps, according to CDA Goes, to keep our municipality livable and attractive.

114616.explanation: Everyone counts, but giving priority to refugees does not promote unity, according to CDA Goes. We choose a fair distribution, without displacement of other groups of housing seekers.

114618.explanation: Agricultural land is needed for food production. That is why CDA Goes first focuses on solar panels on roofs and smart combinations (such as solar panels at parking lots).

114619.explanation: The museum is important for our heritage. CDA Goes does believe renewal is necessary to make the museum future-proof.

114626.explanation: CDA Goes values quality more than just lower rates. Through rate differentiation and good parking facilities, the city remains accessible and welcoming. People gladly pay for convenience and safety. In this way, we invest in a vital city center for entrepreneurs and visitors.

114628.explanation: Additional hotel rooms may be needed at another location, but according to CDA Goes, not at this site. Spatial quality and the environment are leading for us.

114629.explanation: The housing shortage calls for more homes. Besides building in the city, expansion is also needed according to CDA Goes, with attention to livability and facilities.

114632.explanation: Culture connects people and makes the city attractive and lively. Investing in culture is for CDA Goes investing in livability.

114572.explanation: The municipal council is a democratically elected body.

114573.explanation: We support the Right to Challenge. If citizens can demonstrably do better, then we are in favor.

114575.explanation: Entrepreneurs must be able to do business. We agree with this statement provided that there is no nuisance to the surroundings.

114576.explanation: Yes, facilitate and encourage but do not directly provide financial support.

114578.explanation: We must provide shelter to a limited extent/number. Nuisance must be prevented/combated.

114580.explanation: Combat nuisance, sensible heating should be possible.

114582.explanation: is not a government task. Leave it to, for example, an initiative from a village, sports, or neighborhood association.

114585.explanation: Responsibility of the entrepreneur themselves, not a government task.

114587.explanation: Local broadcasters are of great importance, including in emergencies. The municipality receives funds from the national government for the local broadcaster. If additional funds are needed to carry out this emergency task, this is certainly negotiable.

114591.explanation: At the moment, the enforcement team's capacity is adequate; however, efficiency and optimization of this team can be considered, with safety being an important aspect.

114592.explanation: Camera surveillance should be a dynamic tool and therefore flexibly deployable in places that currently require attention.

114594.explanation: In terms of safety, this can be an addition, but the most important thing is that the team radiates and exercises authority. If the baton complements this to strengthen it, then we are in favor.

114595.explanation: If these home seekers are socially or economically bound to the municipality, we consider this a good proposal.

114597.explanation: Not necessary because this sidelines private landlords. In addition, private landlords provide housing types that social housing corporations do not have in their portfolio.

114598.explanation: No subsidy, but as a municipality, we must facilitate and support where necessary.

114601.explanation: Completely stopping this does not seem realistic. However, we see no benefit in further financial support through remission or additional schemes. Self-reliance is important, also for self-esteem. Where really necessary, the municipality should step in.

114602.explanation: We have very good poverty policies and schemes in the municipality of Goes. We do not consider it wise to raise the income limit any further.

114604.explanation: As a municipality, we should specifically facilitate increasing the provision of charging facilities in the neighborhood and at home.

114605.explanation: 50 km/h within built-up areas and 30 km/h in residential neighborhoods is a good compromise between safety and traffic flow.

114608.explanation: It is about the quality care provided by a caregiver; this is independent of who provides the care.

114609.explanation: Price obviously plays a role, but the quality of youth care is essential. So yes, pay attention to the price in combination with quality.

114610.explanation: The municipality already has so many minimum income schemes that we consider this unnecessary.

114613.explanation: Let's be careful with recreational stays and tourists because our local economy largely depends on them.

114616.explanation: People with a residence permit are ordinary citizens with equal rights and therefore receive no preferential treatment.

114618.explanation: As long as there is space on the roofs, sacrificing farmland for solar power is unnecessary.

114619.explanation: Continuing on the current path seems pointless to us. However, we do believe that after a change in course, the budget can be maintained.

114626.explanation: There should be greater rate differentiation where the further from the center, the more affordable it is.

114628.explanation: We should have done this 10 years ago.

114629.explanation: There are sufficient opportunities for additional new residential areas where action can be taken.

114632.explanation: We do not want to cut spending on culture but want to use the money as effectively as possible. We expect cultural institutions to account for how they spend taxpayers' money. We expect cultural institutions to work professionally on their visibility.

114572.explanation: We are against referenda because we have confidence in the people we have elected to make decisions for us. The municipal council is chosen by the residents to make important choices. Referenda can make it harder to make good decisions and cause unrest.

114573.explanation: We find it important that residents can think along and participate in their own living environment. If they can take over the management of a sports hall, park, or other facilities, this increases involvement and ownership. This strengthens the community and ensures that facilities better meet

114575.explanation: We disagree that cafes and restaurants can determine their own opening hours. Free opening hours can lead to more nuisance for residents, such as noise and disturbance of peace. Therefore, we believe the municipality should set rules to maintain livability and safety.

114576.explanation: We think it's good that residents who want to generate sustainable energy together receive financial support from the municipality. That support should be such that it encourages residents to act sustainably and that the funds eventually return to the municipality. This way, we invest together.

114578.explanation: We do not agree that asylum shelters in our municipality should close as soon as possible. We want to help those who are truly fleeing and in need of assistance. In doing so, we pay extra attention to Christian refugees who are persecuted because of their faith.

114580.explanation: We believe that the use of open fireplaces and wood stoves in Goes should remain permitted. However, we do believe this should be done responsibly, according to the heating guide, to protect air quality and residents' health.

114582.explanation: We agree with organizing a fireworks show on New Year's Eve. However, we prefer a general fireworks ban due to the nuisance, environmental impact, and safety. Therefore, we support a fireworks-free Goes.

114585.explanation: We believe it is the responsibility of entrepreneurs themselves to make their buildings accessible to people with disabilities. The municipality can encourage and support them, for example with permit applications, but financial assistance is not a municipal task.

114587.explanation: We do not agree with additional financial support for the local broadcaster by the municipality. Funding comes through national resources, and additional municipal contributions are therefore unnecessary.

114591.explanation: We do not agree with increasing the number of municipal enforcement officers. The current staffing is adequate and provides sufficient capacity to ensure safety and enforcement.

114592.explanation: We do not agree with expanding camera surveillance in Goes. At the moment, all important locations are already well covered, and the current supervision is sufficient to guarantee safety.

114594.explanation: We do not agree with equipping BOAs (officials with enforcement authority) with batons, but do support the use of pepper spray. Safety is important and paramount, but the use of means of violence requires careful consideration. We believe BOAs should be trained in de-escalation, and trust in enforcement must be central.

114595.explanation: We believe that housing seekers from our own municipality should be given priority in the allocation of social rental housing. This ensures that our own residents have a fair chance at affordable housing close to family, work, and social networks.

114597.explanation: We believe that anyone who buys a home in our municipality must live in it themselves. This prevents homes from being bought for rental or speculation purposes, making the housing market fairer and more accessible to residents.

114598.explanation: We do not agree with subsidizing the conversion of vacant offices into housing by the municipality. This is a task for the market and should be arranged without government support. However, the government can cooperate through regulations and permits.

114601.explanation: We do not agree with completely stopping the waiving of municipal taxes for residents with a low income. We do believe sustainable and fair solutions should be sought so that those who truly need it continue to receive appropriate support.

114602.explanation: We believe the current income threshold for poverty assistance in Goes is adequate. Instead of raising it, we want to primarily look for other forms of support to better help people in financial difficulty.

114604.explanation: We do not agree with designating more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars. It is important that this is done efficiently and purposefully, taking into account the needs of residents and businesses.

114605.explanation: We do not agree with a general reduction of the maximum speed to 30 km/h within built-up areas. There are places where 50 km/h is safe and responsible. Therefore, we believe speed should be assessed depending on the situation.

114608.explanation: We find it important that people who need care can always choose a care provider that aligns with their faith, culture, or beliefs. This ensures respect and trust in care, contributing to better support and well-being.

114609.explanation: We do not agree with the idea that the municipality should primarily focus on price when selecting organizations for youth care. Quality of care and a good match with the needs of children and families are far more important than cost alone.

114610.explanation: We do not agree with the free distribution of tampons and sanitary pads by the municipality. This responsibility should lie with the user themselves or through other organizations.

114613.explanation: We do not agree with increasing the tourist tax. The current rates are appropriate, and an increase may reduce Goes' attractiveness as a tourist destination.

114616.explanation: We do not agree with automatically prioritizing refugees with residency permits for social rental housing. Allocation should be based on urgency and the actual housing needs of all housing seekers.

114618.explanation: We believe solar panels on agricultural land should be prohibited. We prefer installation on roofs, so that valuable farmland is preserved for food production and nature.

114619.explanation: We do not agree with stopping financial support for the Historical Museum of De Bevelanden. However, we do believe a new and future-proof concept should be sought to keep the museum attractive and affordable.

114626.explanation: We choose neither. The costs for paid parking in the city center should be kept as low as possible to remain affordable for residents and visitors, without compromising accessibility and quality of life.

114628.explanation: We agree that the municipality should grant permission for the construction of a 55-meter hotel tower on Deltaweg. This fits with Goes’ ambitions and contributes to the attractiveness and economic development of the municipality.

114629.explanation: We do not agree with building a new residential district outside Goes. We prefer developing Mannee-Zuid to invest specifically in existing areas and properly manage the living environment.

114632.explanation: We do not agree with the idea that the municipality should spend more money on culture. A lot is already invested in Goes, and we believe the current level matches needs and possibilities well.

114572.explanation: A simple yes or no does not give an accurate picture of the problems ahead.

114573.explanation: Yes, residents should have that option, for example via the right to challenge. We would like to see residents make more use of this.

114575.explanation: Only nuisance can be a reason to impose closing times after all.

114576.explanation: They can make use of existing or possibly new subsidy schemes.

114578.explanation: Goes adheres to national agreements. In our view, there is no question of expansion. Other municipalities must first take responsibility.

114580.explanation: Enforcement of this is particularly difficult.

114582.explanation: Taking joint responsibility with entrepreneurs to organize a pleasant New Year's Eve.

114585.explanation: Entrepreneurs have their own responsibility here.

114587.explanation: We adhere to the statutory contribution per resident.

114591.explanation: As the only party, we have advocated increasing the number of enforcement officers. Enforcement is still insufficient in our view.

114592.explanation: The number of mobile cameras should be increased.

114594.explanation: If this increases the safety of the BOAs. They must be trained for this.

114595.explanation: This is appropriate for the current housing shortage.

114597.explanation: Private rental is necessary to meet the needs of housing seekers. However, we do not want vacancies or use as holiday homes.

114598.explanation: Converting offices into homes is a good thing in our opinion. Subsidizing is difficult but possible by lower permit costs and cooperating with zoning plan changes.

114601.explanation: We stand up for people who are financially struggling.

114602.explanation: At 130% of the social minimum, we are already at the maximum.

114604.explanation: The number of electric cars is also increasing.

114605.explanation: The main road structure should remain at fifty. 30 km/h zones can be expanded.

114608.explanation: There is already enough pressure on healthcare without regulating this.

114609.explanation: A good balance between price and quality is necessary. Care must be taken to prevent profiteers who use youth care merely as a cash cow without providing quality.

114610.explanation: This, to our satisfaction, is already part of existing policy.

114613.explanation: They use our infrastructure and all other municipal facilities.

114616.explanation: Here we follow the government's rules regarding status holders.

114618.explanation: Our preference is for optimal use of buildings.

114619.explanation: Not necessarily maintaining a loss-making museum. We have learned from the Tropical Forest.

114628.explanation: The location on Deltaweg does not pose an objection for a hotel tower. However, another location within the municipality may also be possible.

114629.explanation: Build, build, build. The demand is high.

114632.explanation: In our view, the current policy is fine.

114572.explanation: We want residents to truly have influence, especially early on through citizen assemblies and strong village and neighborhood councils. A referendum can sometimes help, but it is not always the best solution.

114573.explanation: If residents or associations can manage something better or smarter, they should be given that chance. The municipality should enable and properly support that.

114575.explanation: Entrepreneurs should be given space, but residents also have a right to peace. We want tailored solutions: freedom where possible and clear rules when there is nuisance.

114576.explanation: Initiatives by residents to jointly generate sustainable energy deserve support. With knowledge, good arrangements, and financial aid where necessary, the municipality stimulates innovation to make sustainability achievable for more people.

114578.explanation: Sheltering people who have to flee is a shared responsibility. Closing ‘as soon as possible’ is inhumane and merely passes the problem on.

114580.explanation: Wood burning often causes unhealthy air. We want to reduce nuisance and fine particles, for example with filters and by helping people choose cleaner solutions.

114582.explanation: A fireworks show is a nice and safer alternative to fireworks on the streets and causes less nuisance, injury, and litter. This position aligns with the motion submitted by D66, PvdA/GreenLeft, and the CDA, in which residents are involved in working towards a safer and more pleasant New Year's Eve.

114585.explanation: Everyone must be able to visit shops and businesses. We assist entrepreneurs where possible with advice and (targeted) support to become more accessible.

114587.explanation: Yes, local journalism is important for democracy and for information close to home. Extra support is possible, with clear agreements about independence.

114591.explanation: If extra action is needed for safety and nuisance, more enforcement is logical. We always combine that with prevention and neighborhood collaboration.

114592.explanation: Cameras are necessary in problem areas, but not everywhere. Privacy is important and there must always be good justification.

114594.explanation: Borough officers are there primarily to be visible, approachable, and accessible as hosts of the city. Therefore, we focus on good training, prevention, and cooperation, and not on carrying a baton.

114595.explanation: We want to assign fairly and transparently and keep room for people who come here to work or study. If necessary, we can steer allocation toward certain target groups.

114597.explanation: A home is for living in, not for speculation. Therefore, we support measures such as purchase protection and mandatory owner-occupancy where possible.

114598.explanation: Vacant offices are an opportunity to quickly create housing. Sometimes subsidies help, but often relaxed rules and faster procedures work better.

114601.explanation: We want to help people with low incomes make ends meet. Debt forgiveness prevents small debts from becoming big problems, reduces stress, and improves daily life. This can pave the way to fully participate again, for example through volunteer work or employment.

114602.explanation: We want to tackle poverty and debt early. By raising the income threshold, we ensure that even people who get into trouble because of rising costs receive timely help.

114604.explanation: We want more charging stations in neighborhoods, villages, and at strategic locations. That makes electric driving possible for more people.

114605.explanation: 30 km/h in residential areas makes streets safer for children and cyclists and reduces noise. We want to implement this by 2028 at the latest.

114608.explanation: Care must fit the person and choice is important. At the same time, we must work with the caregivers available and in an inclusive society ensure care together without excluding people.

114609.explanation: Quality and effectiveness must come first in youth care, not just the lowest price. Cheap can be expensive.

114610.explanation: Menstrual products should not be a luxury. Providing them for free especially helps young people and those with little money.

114613.explanation: Tourism sometimes requires extra facilities. A higher tourist tax is possible, but only if the money clearly goes back to management, toilets, and livability.

114616.explanation: Status holders must be able to live quickly and with dignity, but we must also remain honest and transparent to everyone on the waiting list. We choose a balanced approach and good distribution.

114618.explanation: Solar panels on agricultural land may sometimes be necessary, but we especially want to manage location and quality well. No ban, but smart choices and landscape protection.

114619.explanation: We want to preserve and renew the Bevelands Museum as an interactive storytelling house. That is good for education, identity, and the city center.

114626.explanation: Parking must remain fair and understandable. Sometimes it can be cheaper, but we also use rates to allocate space and keep the city center accessible.

114628.explanation: A hotel tower is a major intervention. We find high-rise buildings appropriate at city entrances, such as near the Zeelandhallen and the food court, but not on the Poelbos side. Whether this is possible on Deltaweg depends on integration, traffic, views, and support.

114629.explanation: Due to the large housing task, building outside Goes may be necessary. This can only happen if investment is simultaneously made in good roads, public transport, and safe cycling routes, and in facilities such as schools, culture, and places to meet each other, because a neighborhood is more than just housing.

114632.explanation: We want Goes to become a cultural hotspot, with space for creators, events, and culture in the city and villages. Appropriate funding should be provided for that.

114572.explanation: A binding referendum is a good tool to make direct democratic decisions.

114573.explanation: Square management is typically something a municipality should handle.

114575.explanation: The more freedom, the better. Of course, it must not cause nuisance.

114576.explanation: The municipality should not subsidize. The market will do its work.

114578.explanation: There must be a complete asylum stop both nationally and in the municipality, and the asylum centers must be closed.

114580.explanation: People should be able to decide for themselves whether to burn wood or not. We need to stop the climate madness.

114582.explanation: Setting off fireworks is a wonderful tradition.

114585.explanation: That should be done by entrepreneurs themselves. The government has no role in this.

114587.explanation: If local broadcasters are not viable, then they should disappear.

114592.explanation: Camera surveillance leads to more government control.

114595.explanation: Conflicts with Article 1 of the Constitution.

114597.explanation: The market must do its work. No overregulation!

114598.explanation: No subsidies. But make transformations easier!

114601.explanation: Forgiving taxes leads to an increase in the poverty trap.

114602.explanation: People should keep more when they go to work.

114604.explanation: There are already enough charging points.

114608.explanation: People should be grateful to receive care.

114609.explanation: Price and quality must be closely monitored.

114610.explanation: People can perfectly well buy this themselves.

114613.explanation: The tourist tax should actually be abolished.

114616.explanation: The Netherlands must stop taking in "refugees".

114618.explanation: Solar panels belong on roofs. Not on agricultural land.

114626.explanation: Parking should be as cheap as possible.

114628.explanation: If there is a need for it and no tax money is wasted, that is fine.

114629.explanation: If there is a need for it, that is fine.

114632.explanation: Culture should support itself. Civil servants and politicians often have no idea what culture is, making it often a big waste of taxpayers' money.

statements.title: A new housing development should be built outside Goes.
The municipality should financially help business owners to make their buildings accessible to people with disabilities.

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

titleParts.text: helpen om hun gebouw toegankelijker te maken voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Something that limits you in daily life. A disability can be physical or intellectual or relate to hearing or sight, or combinations of these.

statements.title: The residents of Goes should be able to give their opinion on important topics via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Goes moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: Cafés and restaurants must be able to determine their own opening hours.
The municipality should support the local broadcaster with additional funding.

titleParts.glossary: Radio and television by and for people from the municipality and the immediate area.

statements.title: Residents should be able to take over the management of, for example, a sports hall or a public green space from the municipality.
The asylum reception center in our municipality should be closed as soon as possible.

titleParts.glossary: A place where people who have applied for asylum are accommodated. An asylum shelter can be temporary. A permanent asylum shelter is called an asylum seekers' centre (azc).

titleParts.text: in onze gemeente moet zo snel mogelijk sluiten.

statements.title: Anyone who buys a home in our municipality must live in it themselves.
Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: In Goes the use of open fireplaces and wood stoves should remain allowed.

titleParts.text: In Goes moet het gebruik van open haarden en

titleParts.glossary: Wood-burning stoves used to heat a home.

statements.title: Vacant offices should be converted into housing with subsidies from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente worden omgebouwd tot woningen.

statements.title: The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
The municipality should grant permission for a hotel tower (55 m) on Deltaweg.
The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
Goes should have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who deal with safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: Goes should have more camera surveillance.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
When allocating social housing, applicants from the municipality should be given priority.

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: moeten woningzoekenden uit de eigen gemeente voorrang krijgen.

statements.title: The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: The municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who carry out safety checks in Goes should be given a baton.

titleParts.glossary: Special Enforcement Officers. They can issue fines and often supervise the streets.

titleParts.text: die in Goes controleren op veiligheid moeten een wapenstok krijgen.

statements.title: The municipality should distribute tampons and sanitary pads for free.
The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet stoppen met het

titleParts.glossary: That people no longer have to pay for something.
Taxes you have to pay to the municipality (e.g., sewer charge, waste disposal charge, dog tax).

titleParts.text: voor inwoners met een laag inkomen.

statements.title: The tourist tax should be increased.

titleParts.glossary: The tax you must pay when you stay overnight in a hotel or at a campsite.

statements.title: When selecting organizations for youth care, the municipality should primarily pay attention to price.
The municipality must continue to give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: Goes should stop giving money to the Historic Museum De Bevelanden.
The municipality should ban solar panels on agricultural land.
Paid parking in the center should be made cheaper.
The municipality should spend more money on culture.
